content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 1,
1992
Kelly Barrales
1116 Foothill Street
Redwood City,
CA 94061
Re:
Your Request For Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1-92-442
Dear Ms. Barrales:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you on behalf of Senator Rebecca Morgan,
concerning the
campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”).l/
Your letter states only a general inquiry.
Therefore,
we consider it to be a request for informal assistance.2/
Your letter, dated July 30,
1992, correctly summarizes my
telephone advice that a candidate does not have to report
contributions which were made by her spouse, if those
contributions were made from the spouse’s legally separate funds.
Please call me at
(916)
322-5662 if you have additional
questions.
Sincerely,
Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
g.
All references to regulations are to Title
2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660

Therefore, we consider it to be a request for informal assistance.2/ Your letter, dated July 30, 1992, correctly summarizes my telephone advice that a candidate does not have to report contributions which were made by her spouse, if those contributions were made from the spouse’s legally separate funds.
